tasks.name: P1: FM2 Continuous Metrics on Full Factorial (H2-revised)
tasks.description: Compute Kendall tau, Spearman rho, and NDCG on raw attribution scores for the 2x2 factorial {TRAK, RepSim} x {standard, contrastive} on all 3 DATE-LM tasks using full training pools (N=5473 counterfact, N=10187 toxicity). Reuse pilot pipeline code for RepSim and TRAK computation. The critical difference from pilot: evaluate with CONTINUOUS metrics (not rank-based) to break the mean-subtraction invariance. Also compute the full method tournament: {RepSim, TRAK, LoGra, DDA, Raw Dot IF, Diag IF, k-NN, BM25} x {standard, contrastive} with both rank-based and continuous metrics.
tasks.comment_gpu: Full training pool attribution requires computing pairwise scores. 2 GPUs parallelize method evaluation (RepSim+baselines on GPU0, TRAK+LoGra on GPU1). N=5473 fits in 24GB with batch processing.

pilot.pass_criteria: Kendall tau computable for all method-task pairs; at least one method shows Kendall tau difference > 0.01 between standard and contrastive scoring (confirms continuous metrics CAN detect differences even if small)

visualizations.description: Full method tournament: rank-based and continuous metrics
2x2 factorial with continuous metrics

tasks.name: P1: Controlled Contamination Injection (H11)
tasks.description: For each method M in {RepSim, TRAK}: (1) compute raw attribution scores s_i, (2) compute mean mu = mean(s_i), (3) inject contamination: s_contaminated = s + alpha * mu for alpha in {0, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0}, (4) apply contrastive correction: s_corrected = s_contaminated - mean(s_contaminated), (5) evaluate contaminated and corrected scores on both rank-based AND continuous metrics. This provides causal FM2 validation independent of any metric choice. Run on counterfact (full N=5473) and toxicity (full N=10187) tasks on Pythia-1B.
tasks.comment_gpu: Reuses pre-computed attribution scores from p1_fm2_continuous_metrics; injection and evaluation are CPU-only. 1 GPU for any score recomputation needed.

pilot.pass_criteria: At alpha=1.0: contaminated performance drops by >= 5pp on R@50; contrastive correction recovers >= 80% of alpha=0 performance. Also: Kendall tau shows monotonic degradation with increasing alpha.

visualizations.description: Contamination injection curves: performance vs alpha
Contrastive recovery curves: corrected performance vs alpha

tasks.name: P1: FM1/FM2 Interaction Analysis (H3-revised)
tasks.description: Aggregate results from p1_fm2_continuous_metrics. Compute 2-way ANOVA on Kendall tau: Factor A = space (parameter vs representation), Factor B = scoring (standard vs contrastive). Compute interaction term as fraction of minimum main effect. Report per-task: main effect FM1, main effect FM2, interaction, eta-squared. CONTINGENT: only meaningful if H2-revised passes (Kendall tau gain >= 0.05 for parameter-space methods).

pilot.pass_criteria: ANOVA computable with nonzero main effects; interaction term < 30% of min(main_effect_FM1, main_effect_FM2) on >= 2/3 tasks

visualizations.description: 2-way ANOVA: FM1 x FM2 interaction on continuous metrics

tasks.name: P2: Full-Scale Eigenspectrum at Multiple N (H4-revised, H9-revised)
tasks.description: On Pythia-70M, compute gradient covariance eigenspectrum via Lanczos (top-500 eigenvalues) at N in {500, 1000, 2000, 5000}. Compute exact representation covariance eigenspectrum (d=512) at each N. Measure r_eff(95%), r_eff(99%), condition number, top-5/10/50 explained variance at each N. Track how r_eff scales with N. At N=5000 (N >> d=512), the representation condition number is reliable. Compute spectral concentration ratio: rep_r_eff/d vs grad_r_eff/B. Pilot showed r_eff=10 (full model, N=100) and r_eff=63 (representation, N=100); full-scale confirms whether these stabilize.
tasks.comment_gpu: Pythia-70M is small enough for single GPU. Lanczos at N=5000 with top-500 eigenvalues ~30 min. Representation eigendecomp ~10 min. Total ~50 min with 4 N values (earlier N values are faster).

pilot.pass_criteria: Lanczos converges at N=500; r_eff(95%) for full-model gradients remains < 100; r_eff increases with N but remains << B

visualizations.description: Eigenspectrum: gradient vs representation (log-log) at N=5000
r_eff scaling with sample size N
Spectral statistics across N values

tasks.name: P2: TRAK Dimension Sweep at Full Scale (H5-revised)
tasks.description: Run TRAK on Pythia-1B with full counterfact training set (N=5473). Random projection at k in {32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096}. PCA projection (onto top-k gradient eigenvectors) at k in {32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048}. Evaluate with BOTH rank-based (R@50, MRR) AND continuous (Kendall tau) metrics. Smoking gun: TRAK-PCA at k=d=2048 vs RepSim gap. Pilot showed peak at k=256, non-monotonic after, and 30.8pp gap at k=d.
tasks.comment_gpu: 15 total runs (8 random + 7 PCA). 2 GPUs run in parallel: ~4 runs per GPU per hour with N=5473. PCA projection reuses eigenvectors from p2_eigenspectrum_fullscale (or recomputes on Pythia-1B target layers).

pilot.pass_criteria: Saturation knee visible at k in [d/16, d/4]; TRAK-PCA outperforms TRAK-random at same k on at least 4/7 k values

visualizations.description: TRAK dimension sweep: R@50 vs k (random vs PCA, full scale)
TRAK dimension sweep: Kendall tau vs k

tasks.name: P2: RepSim PCA Dimension Sweep at Full Scale
tasks.description: Run RepSim on Pythia-1B with PCA dimension reduction at k in {16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048} for all 3 DATE-LM tasks at full scale (N=5473 counterfact, N=10187 toxicity). Identify minimum k for RepSim to maintain near-full performance (within 3pp of k=d). Pilot showed saturation at k=64 (limited by N=100). Full scale should reveal the true saturation point.
tasks.comment_gpu: Representations cached from p1_fm2_continuous_metrics or recomputed once. PCA sweep is CPU-dominated after extraction. 1 GPU sufficient.

pilot.pass_criteria: RepSim at k=d matches full RepSim within 1pp; graceful degradation with knee at k in [d/8, d/2]

visualizations.description: RepSim PCA dimension reduction: R@50 vs k (all tasks)

tasks.name: P3: Dense Retrieval Baselines -- Contriever + GTR-T5 (H8-revised)
tasks.description: Download and run Contriever (facebook/contriever) and GTR-T5 (sentence-transformers/gtr-t5-base) on all 3 DATE-LM tasks at full scale. Encode all training and test samples with each retrieval model. Compute cosine similarity scores. Evaluate with same metric suite as TDA methods (R@50, MRR, AUPRC, Kendall tau). Also run BM25 at full scale (N=5473 counterfact, N=10187 toxicity). Critical test: if retrieval models match RepSim, 'attribution = retrieval' and paper must reposition.
tasks.comment_gpu: Contriever and GTR-T5 are small encoder models (<500M params). Encoding N=10K samples takes ~10 min each. BM25 is CPU-only. Total ~40 min on 1 GPU.

pilot.pass_criteria: Both retrieval models produce valid scores; at least one of {Contriever, GTR} shows > 5pp gap below RepSim on >= 1 task

visualizations.description: Retrieval baselines vs RepSim comparison

tasks.name: P4: Gap Decomposition -- TRAK-PCA vs RepSim (H10)
tasks.description: Systematically decompose the 30.8pp gap between TRAK-PCA at k=d and RepSim. Factor (a): Last-layer-only TRAK-PCA -- restrict gradients to final transformer layer only, then random/PCA project. Factor (b): Cosine-normalized TRAK-PCA -- L2-normalize gradient vectors before projection. Factor (c): Combined -- last-layer-only + cosine-normalized. Factor (d): Residual -- whatever gap remains is attributed to nonlinear semantic features / method architecture. All on Pythia-1B, full counterfact (N=5473). Report residual gap after each factor.
tasks.comment_gpu: 3 TRAK variants (last-layer, cosine-norm, combined) each at k=d=2048. Last-layer gradients are much cheaper to compute. 1 GPU sufficient.

pilot.pass_criteria: At least one factor (a or b) reduces gap by >= 5pp; combined (a+b) reduces gap by >= 10pp

visualizations.description: Gap decomposition: factor contributions
Gap decomposition detail

tasks.name: P4: RepSim Layer Sweep
tasks.description: Run RepSim at layers {0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 23} on Pythia-1B for all 3 DATE-LM tasks at full scale. Identify which layers carry attribution signal for each task type. Expected: later layers better for semantic tasks (counterfact, ftrace), possibly earlier/middle layers for behavioral (toxicity). This informs the task-type boundary characterization (RQ3).
tasks.comment_gpu: 7 layers x 3 tasks = 21 evaluations. Representation extraction at each layer ~5 min, reused across tasks. 1 GPU sufficient.

pilot.pass_criteria: Performance varies by >= 10pp across layers on at least 1 task; last layer is best on at least 1 semantic task

visualizations.description: RepSim layer sweep: performance by layer and task
RepSim performance vs layer depth per task

tasks.name: P4: Cosine vs Euclidean RepSim (Contrarian Test)
tasks.description: Run RepSim with Euclidean distance (L2 distance instead of cosine similarity) on all 3 DATE-LM tasks at full scale on Pythia-1B. Contrarian falsification: if Euclidean matches cosine on all tasks, then normalization is NOT a factor in the TRAK-PCA vs RepSim gap. If cosine >> Euclidean, normalization is a key factor (gradient norms carry non-attribution noise). Also test dot product (no normalization) as third variant.
tasks.comment_gpu: Reuses cached representations. Metric computation is CPU-dominated. 1 GPU for representation extraction if not cached.

pilot.pass_criteria: All three similarity functions produce valid scores; cosine vs Euclidean gap > 3pp on at least 1 task (normalization matters)

visualizations.description: Similarity function comparison: cosine vs Euclidean vs dot product

tasks.name: P5: PCA-Reduced Whitened Attribution (H7-revised)
tasks.description: Implement whitened RepSim in PCA-reduced subspace: project representations to top-k PCA components, then whiten with the k x k covariance matrix in that subspace. k in {16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512}. At N=5473 and k=64, N/k=85 (well-determined). Also test ridge-regularized whitening: M = (Sigma + lambda*I)^{-1} with lambda from 5-fold cross-validation. Compare against standard RepSim (M=I) on all 3 tasks. Pilot showed M=Sigma^{-1} fails at full d=2048 (N/d=0.049) but SNR-accuracy correlation was positive (r=0.34), suggesting the concept is sound at feasible N/k ratios.
tasks.comment_gpu: PCA + whitening is CPU-dominated after representation extraction. 6 k values x 3 tasks + ridge cross-validation. 1 GPU sufficient.

pilot.pass_criteria: PCA-whitened at k=64 does not degrade RepSim by > 5pp (unlike full-dimensional whitening which lost 8-11pp); positive SNR-accuracy correlation maintained

visualizations.description: PCA-whitened RepSim: performance vs subspace dimension k
PCA-whitened vs standard RepSim comparison
Per-query SNR vs attribution accuracy at optimal k

tasks.name: Final Analysis: Decision Gate Evaluation and Paper Positioning
tasks.description: Aggregate all results from P1-P5. Evaluate all decision gates: (1) FM2 Tier 1 or theoretical? (2) Attribution vs retrieval boundary? (3) PCA whitening rescued? Compute final hypothesis scorecard with updated status for H1-H11. Generate paper-ready tables and figure data. Determine final paper positioning: which contributions are Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3. Write structured summary for result_debate stage.

pilot.pass_criteria: All decision gates evaluated; hypothesis scorecard complete; paper positioning determined

visualizations.description: Final hypothesis scorecard: H1-H11 status after full experiments

metadata.revision_basis: Post-pilot revision. Pilot (iter_001) completed 14 tasks in ~28 min. Key changes: continuous metrics for FM2, retrieval baselines, gap decomposition, PCA whitening.

phases.purpose: FM2 Verification Suite -- test FM2 with continuous metrics and controlled injection
FM1 Diagnostic Suite -- full-scale eigenspectrum and dimension sweeps
Retrieval Baselines -- test attribution vs retrieval boundary
Mechanism Suite -- decompose TRAK-PCA vs RepSim gap

decision_gates.condition: Kendall tau gain >= 0.05 for TRAK standard -> TRAK contrastive on >= 1 task AND contamination injection recovery >= 90% at alpha=1.0
decision_gates.if_true: FM2 is Tier 1 contribution; strengthen two-defect narrative
decision_gates.if_false: FM2 demoted to theoretical hypothesis; paper narrows to FM1 + systematic benchmark

decision_gates.condition: Contriever or GTR-T5 matches RepSim (< 3pp gap) on >= 2 tasks
decision_gates.if_true: Reposition from 'attribution quality' to 'attribution vs retrieval boundary analysis'
decision_gates.if_false: RepSim captures genuine model-internal attribution beyond retrieval; proceed with current framing

decision_gates.condition: Combined (last-layer + cosine-norm) TRAK-PCA closes gap to within 10pp of RepSim
decision_gates.if_true: Gap is primarily engineering (layer + normalization); FM1 is the dominant mechanism
decision_gates.if_false: Nonlinear semantic features dominate; FM1 is necessary but not sufficient

decision_gates.condition: PCA-whitened at any k outperforms standard RepSim by >= 3pp on any task
decision_gates.if_true: Matched filter theory rescued; framework is prescriptive
decision_gates.if_false: Honest negative; framework is taxonomic/organizational only
